title: Sarepta Therapeutics SWOT Analysis - DMD Leader's Stock Poised for Growth
text:
A comprehensive SWOT analysis of Sarepta Therapeutics reveals a company well-positioned in the DMD therapeutics market, though facing both significant opportunities and risks. The SWOT framework helps identify key investment considerations for SRPT shares.

Strengths: First-mover advantage with ELEVIDYS, the first approved gene therapy for DMD. Multiple approved therapies reducing single-product risk (EXONDYS 51, VYONDYS 53, AMONDYS 45). Achieved profitability and positive cash flow, reducing financing risk. Large addressable market (15,000 U.S. DMD patients). Strong management team with track record of execution.

Weaknesses: ELEVIDYS label restrictions limited near-term market opportunity. EMBARK primary endpoint miss despite secondary endpoint success created uncertainty. Gene therapy safety profile requires ongoing monitoring. Dependence on DMD market for majority of revenue. High manufacturing complexity and costs for gene therapy.

Opportunities: Label expansion to non-ambulatory patients (ENDEAVOR Cohort 8 trial). International expansion of ELEVIDYS and PMO therapies. Pipeline expansion to other genetic muscular dystrophies (SRP-9003, SRP-9004, SRP-9005). Peak sales estimates of $2.7-5 billion for ELEVIDYS. Market growth as DMD diagnosis rates improve.

Threats: Competitive gene therapy programs from other companies (Pfizer, Roche). Regulatory restrictions on ELEVIDYS due to safety concerns. Reimbursement and pricing pressure. Patient hesitancy due to safety perceptions. Patent expiration risk on PMO franchise. Economic downturn reducing treatment initiation rates.
